Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: http://bimerr.iot.linkeddata.es/def/building#Space
Label: Space
Definition: A space represents an area or volume bounded actually or theoretically. Spaces are areas or volumes that provide for certain functions within a building.

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4inma_2019-04-30.n3 (CORE)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/saref4inma/Area
Label: Area
Definition: A subclass of s4bldg:BuildingSpace used to define the physical spaces of the building. According to IEC 62264, areas are physical, geographical or logical groupings of resources determined by the site. A site can be divided in areas, whereas areas contain work centers.s.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: Zóna
Definition: 区域是物理世界或虚拟世界的一部分，既位于现实空间中又可在3D空间中拓展。bot:Zone的子集包括bot:Site, bot:Building, bot:Storey, or bot:Space。bot:Zone实例可包含其他bot:Zone实例，从而可以对区域进行分组或细分。 同时，bot：Zone的实例也可以与其他bot：Zone实例相邻。最后，bot：Zone可以实例化为bot：Element的两个关系，它们可以包含在（bot：containsElement）中，或者与其相邻（bot：adjacentElement）。

Concept: Class
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: (1) a separately controlled heated or cooled space. (2) one occupied space or several occupied spaces with similar occupancy category, occupant density, zone air distribution effectiveness, and zone primary airflow per unit area. (3) space or group of spaces within a building for which the heating, cooling, or lighting requirements are sufficiently similar that desired conditions can be maintained throughout by a single controlling device.

Concept: Class
Ontology: th_building.owl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/thinkhome/ontology/BuildingOntology.owl#Area
Label: Area
Definition: Specifies the area of the space or building. This value should be equal to the area enclosed by the physical boundaries of the space, as defined by the intersection of the horizontal plane at the highest floor height and the volumetric model's vertical interior surface planes. Building area should be the sum of space areas whose PeopleNumber element values are greater than zero.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasElement
Label: has element
Definition: Links a Zone to an Element that is either contained in, adjacent to or intersecting with the Zone. The intended use of this relationship is not to be stated explicitly, but to be inferred from its sub-properties. It will, for example, allow one to query for all the doors of a building given that they have an adjacency to spaces of the building.

Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#intersectingElement
Label: intersecting element
Definition: Relation between a Zone and a building Element that intersects it.

URI: https://w3id.org/bot#adjacentElement
Label: adjacent element
Definition: 区域中相邻建筑构件之间的关系，以该区域为界。

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#containsZone
Label: enthält Zone
Definition: رابطه با زیر منطقه های یک منطقه اصلی. به عنوان مثال یک منطقه فضایی می تواند در یک منطقه طبقه قرار گرفته باشد که آن به خودی خود در یک منطقه ساختمان قرار گرفته. رابطه‌ی  bot:containsZone یک رابطه‌ی انتقالی است. به این معنا که در مثال قبلی رابطه‌ی bot:containsZone بین بخش فضا و بخش ساختمان نیز برقرار میشود.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: 区域
Definition: بخشی از دنیای فیزیکی، و یا مجازی که اساساً در دنیا وجود دارد و دارای حدود مکانی سه بعدی است. زیرکلاس‌های bot:Zone عبارتند از bot:Site، bot:Building، bot:Storey، وbot:Space.
 یک عنصر bot:Zone میتواند دربرگیرنده‌ی عناصر دیگری از جنس bot:Zone  باشد و به این گونه امکان گروه بندی و جداسازی مناطق ختلف بوجود می‌آید. یک عنصر bot:Zone می‌تواند هم‌جوار عناصر دیگری از جنس bot:Zone باشد. در پایان، یک عنصر bot:Zone می‌تواند آغازگر دو نوع رابطه با عناصر bot:Element باشد که شامل دربرگیرندگی (bot:containsElement) وهم‌جواری  (bot:adjacentElement)  است.

Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasElement
Label: tem elemento
Definition: Links a Zone to an Element that is either contained in, adjacent to or intersecting with the Zone. The intended use of this relationship is not to be stated explicitly, but to be inferred from its sub-properties. It will, for example, allow one to query for all the doors of a building given that they have an adjacency to spaces of the building.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Interface
Label: Interface
Definition: A generic concept to qualify the relationship of two or more things in the world, where at least one is a building element or zone. Examples:
    - Qualification of heat transmission between zones through one or more building elements. This includes one-dimensional (surface) heat losses from one zone to another through a single building element, a two dimensional (line) loss from one zone to another through the connection in which the two elements meet or a three dimensional (point) loss from one zone to another through the connection where three elements (typically two walls and a slab) meet. 
    - Connection of an electric device to the electric system of a building. 
    - A door between one room and another.

Label: Material
Definition: Material is a homogeneous or inhomogeneous substance that can be used to form elements (physical products or their components). Material is the basic entity for material designation and definition; this includes identification by name and classification (via reference to an external classification). Material must have a name (=material grade e.g. S355JR) and a category (=material e.g. Steel, Aluminium etc.). Material defines the planned/proposed material. The finally used material is stored in the property set.

building.ttl: Zones are areas with spatial 3D volumes, and include, Buildings, Storeys, and Spaces.
Zones may contain other zones, Buildings may contain storeys, Storeys may contain spaces.
There are building elements, which may have sub elements.
s4inma_2019-04-30.n3: Zones are areas with spatial 3D volumes, and include, Buildings, Storeys, and Spaces.
bot.ttl: Zones are areas with spatial 3D volumes, and include, Buildings, Storeys, and Spaces.
Zones may intersect, or be adjacent to other zones.
Zones may have elements, either contained, adjacent, or intersecting it.
A adjacent zones and/or elements share some interface.
Zones and Elements have a 3D Model (including geometry, material, etc.).
brick.ttl: Zones are areas with spatial 3D volumes, and include, Buildings, Storeys, and Spaces.
There are building elements, which may have sub elements.
beo.ttl: There are building elements, which may have sub elements.

veredicto_final:
**Veredicto Final**

Recomendación de Red: **building.ttl**, **bot.ttl**, and **brick.ttl**

Justificación:

1. **building.ttl**: Esta ontología cubre los conceptos básicos de edificios, como zonas, edificios, plantas y espacios. Es fundamental para entender la estructura y organización de un edificio.
2. **bot.ttl**: Esta ontología agrega información adicional sobre las relaciones entre zonas y elementos, como la posibilidad de que dos zonas se intersecten o sean adyacentes. También describe los modelos 3D de zonas y elementos, lo que es crucial para entender la geometría y materialidad de un edificio.
3. **brick.ttl**: Esta ontología complementa a las otras dos, proporcionando información sobre la estructura de los elementos constructivos y su relación con las zonas. Esto ayuda a comprender cómo se organizan los materiales y componentes en un edificio.

Observaciones:

* La cobertura de requisitos es generalmente buena en esta recomendación, ya que cubre conceptos como zonas, edificios, plantas, espacios, elementos constructivos y relaciones entre ellos.
* Sin embargo, hay algunos requisitos no cubiertos, como la relación entre los materiales y componentes (no mencionado en la matriz de cobertura). Esto podría ser un área de investigación adicional para completar la cobertura.
* La ontología **s4inma_2019-04-30.n3** no se incluyó en la recomendación debido a que su cobertura es similar a la de **building.ttl**, y no agrega información significativamente nueva.
